<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山辺の道]]

{{Citation needed|date=June 8, 2012}}
{{Expand Japanese}}
[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi02s3200.jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi near {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}'s {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}.]]
[[File:山の辺の道 - panoramio (1).jpg|thumb|280px|Yamanobe-no-Michi]]

'''Yamanobe-no-Michi''' (山辺の道, {{small|Ancient Pronunciation}}: Yamanobe-no-Michi, {{small|Classical Writing}}: '''山辺道''', {{small|Old-Character Style}}: '''山邊道''') is one of {{ill|Japan's ancient roads|ja|日本の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's ancient roads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a representative example of {{ill|Yamato's ancient roads|ja|大和の古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato's ancient roads|1=Q11433382|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433382}}. It refers to a road that traversed the foothills of ancient {{ill|Yamato|ja|大和|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato|1=Yamato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q13417196}} (a region known as Yamanobe, the origin of {{ill|Yamabe District, Nara|en|Yamabe District, Nara|WD=Q1130739|lt=Yamanobe District|1=Yamabe District, Nara}}). It is recognized as the '''oldest road in Japanese history''' and as the '''oldest existing road in Japan'''{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2001|p=82}}{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

The road runs from the foothills of {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa|1=Mount Miwa}}, in the southeast of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}, to the foothills of {{ill|Kasugayama|en|Kasugayama|WD=Q11513600|lt=Kasuga Mountain|1=Kasugayama}} in the northeast. It follows the eastern edge of the basin, skirting the foothills along the Kasuga Fault Escarpment in a north-south direction.

Though some local government authorities use the variant spelling "Yama-no-be no Michi" (山の辺の道), it does not adhere to historical or {{ill|Toponymy|en|Toponymy|WD=Q485762|lt=toponymy|1=Toponymy}} conventions. In contrast, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} uses the classical spelling "Yamanobe-michi" or "Yamabe-michi"{{efn|Example: The Imperial Household Agency manages {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} such as {{ill|Andon'yama Kofun|ja|行燈山古墳|1=Andon-yama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon'yama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Andon-yama Kofun|qq=}}, officially referred to as "Yamabe-michi Makari-no-Oka Kami-no-Misasagi" (山辺道勾岡上陵)}}.

== Overview ==

The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} includes the following reference in the section about Emperor Sujin:

{{Quotation|The imperial mausoleum was constructed along the Yamabe-no-Michi.|Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin Chapter}}

This indicates that Yamanobe-no-Michi was established before Emperor Sujin's mausoleum was built.

Additionally, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} contains similar descriptions in its accounts of Emperors Sujin and Keikō. These descriptions further support the idea that Yamanobe-no-Michi had already been established during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (4th century CE){{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=136}}.

Archaeological findings suggest that it may have existed as early as the late {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}}, connecting the {{ill|Furu Ruins|ja|布留遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furu Ruins|qq=}} and {{ill|Makimuku ruins|ja|纏向遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku ruins|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=纒向遺跡}}.

[[File:Yamanobe-no-michi01s3872.jpg|thumb|270px|A monument inscribed with a {{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}} poem by {{ill|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|en|Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|WD=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}}. Located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}}.]]
[[File:Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto) Kagehime Poem.jpg|thumb|180px|A monument commemorating Kagehime's song along the road near {{ill|Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|lt=Wanishita Shrine|ja|和爾下神社_(天理市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wanishita Shrine (Tenri)|1=Wanishita Shrine (Ichinomoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59282589}} in Tenri City.]]

Stretching approximately 35 kilometers and measuring less than 2 meters wide, the road passes through key historical sites such as {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}, {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and the {{ill|Japanese imperial tombs|en|Japanese imperial tombs|WD=Q17218816|lt=Japanese imperial tombs|1=Japanese imperial tombs}}. These features highlight its importance as a cultural and political thoroughfare{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|pp=137–138}}{{sfn|Asai Kenji|2015|p=107}}.

=== Course ===

The modern hiking route, approximately 15 kilometers long, is a part of the {{ill|Tōkai_Nature_Trail|en|Tōkai_Nature_Trail|WD=Q4249812|lt=Tōkai Nature Trail|1=Tōkai Nature Trail}}. It is particularly popular between Isonokami Shrine in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|WD=Q502196|lt=Tenri|1=Tenri, Nara}} and Ōmiwa Shrine in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai|1=Sakurai, Nara}}. The northern section of the ancient road is less defined due to erosion over time{{sfn|Rom International (ed.)|2005|p=137}}.

== Notable Sites and Historic Landmarks Along the Route ==
* {{ill|Isonokami Shrine|en|Isonokami Shrine|WD=Q708859|lt=Isonokami Shrine|1=Isonokami Jingū}}
* {{ill|Ruins of Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|ja|内山永久寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ruins of Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|1=Uchiyama Eikyū-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393985}}
* {{ill|Ōyamato Shrine|en|Ōyamato Shrine|WD=Q245731|lt=Yamato Shrine|1=Ōyamato Shrine}}
* {{ill|Nishiyamazuka Kofun|ja|西山塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyamazuka Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Chōgaku-ji|en|Chōgaku-ji|WD=Q11652171|lt=Chōgaku-ji|1=Chōgaku-ji}}
* {{ill|Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomb of Emperor Sujin|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=崇神天皇|1=Emperor Sujin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q333530}}
* {{ill|Kurozuka Kofun|en|Kurozuka Kofun|WD=Q1793171|lt=Kurozuka Kofun|1=Kurozuka Kofun}}
* {{ill|Kushiyama Kofun|en|Kushiyama Kofun|WD=Q11544664|lt=Kushiyama Kofun|1=Kushiyama Kofun}}
* {{ill|Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|大和天神山古墳|lt=Tenjinyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}}
* {{ill|Shibuya Mukoyama Kofun|ja|渋谷向山古墳|lt=Tomb of Emperor Keikō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibuya Mukoyama Kofun|1=Shibutani Mukoyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561706}}
* {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|en|Hashihaka Kofun|WD=Q11603420|lt=Hashihaka Kofun|1=Hashihaka Kofun}}
* {{ill|Hibara Shrine|ja|檜原神社|lt=Hibara Shrine (Ōmiwa Shrine Area)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hibara Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=大神神社|1=Ōmiwa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q705542}}
* {{ill|Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|ja|穴師坐兵主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anashi Izasu Hyozu Shrine|1=Anashi-Nimasu Hyōzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597229}}
* {{ill|Yatogi Shrine|ja|夜都伎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatogi Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=夜都岐神社|1=Yatsugi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431328}}
* {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Mount Miwa|1=Mount Miwa}}
* {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}
* {{ill|Takenouchi Moated Settlement|ja|竹之内環濠集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenouchi Moated Settlement|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Kayō Moated Settlement|ja|萱生環濠集落|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kayō Moated Settlement|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* {{ill|Shiki Mizugaki Palace|ja|磯城瑞籬宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki Mizugaki Palace|1=Shiki Mizugaki Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11587954}}

==Notes==
<references group="lower-alpha"/>

== References ==
* {{Cite book|author=Asai Kenji|title=Dictionary of Roads and Paths|publisher=Nihon Jitsugyo Shuppansha|date=2001|isbn=4-534-03315-X}}
* {{Cite book|author=Rom International (ed.)|title=Knowledge of Roads and History|publisher=Kawade Shobō|date=2005|isbn=4-309-49566-4}}

{{translated page|ja|山辺の道|version=98030738|comment=Imported full ja history}}



[[ja:山辺の道]]

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
[[it:Yamanobe no Michi]]


{{wikidata link|Q11470977}}
[[Category:Footpaths]]
[[Category:Roads in Nara Prefecture]]
[[Category:Transportation History of Nara Prefecture]]
[[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
[[Category:Yamato-Aogaki Quasi-National Park]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
